---
title: "Lecture 20: International Law on Trial"
subtitle: "PSCI 3328 — International Relations"
author: "Giuseppe Peressotti"
institute: "University of Texas at Dallas"
date: "04/08/2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, template_B_bold.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    hash: true
    center: false
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0.05
    navigation-mode: linear
    controls: true
    chalkboard: true
    footer: "PSCI 3328 — International Relations | Giuseppe Peressotti"
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#1B2838"
---


## Attendance {.center}

![](pictures/qr.png){height=700}



## Recap + Today

Monday: why international law and organizations exist, and why powerful states sometimes ignore them.

**The big tension:** states follow international law most of the time, but when it conflicts with their interests, they often don't. So does it actually *work*?

Today we delve into **six cases from the last year** (related to some institution in one way or another)


## Today's Activity

**International Law on Trial:** six articles, six institutions, six verdicts.

1. Each pair of groups gets the same article
2. Read together, discuss, reach a verdict
3. One group presents, the other jumps in, challenges, or adds
4. Class discussion


## {.section-slide background-color="#1B2838"}

::: {.section-slide}
# The Six Cases
:::


## The Articles

| Institution | Article |
|-------------|---------|
| **ICC** | "Duterte, Netanyahu, and Putin: The ICC's Power Play" (*Opinio Juris*, Apr 2025) |
| **ICJ** | "On Palestine and the Death of the West's International Legal Order" (*EJIL:Talk!*, Nov 2025) |
| **UN Security Council** | "UN Security Council: Reform or Irrelevance" (*CIVICUS*, Jan 2026) |
| **WTO** | "A Year After 'Liberation Day': The Costs of Trump's Tariffs" (*CFR*, Apr 2026) |
| **WHO** | "The U.S. Has Exited the WHO: What That Means" (*U.S. News*, Jan 2026) |
| **Climate Law** | "A Landmark Court Ruling Looms Over U.S. Absence at COP30" (*Inside Climate News*, Nov 2025) |


## {.section-slide background-color="#1B2838"}

::: {.section-slide}
# Phase 1: Read and Discuss

20 minutes
:::


## Your Discussion Questions

Work through these as a group:

1. **What is actually happening?** Summarize the situation in 2–3 sentences. Who are the key actors, and what is at stake?

2. **Is the institution doing its job?** What was it supposed to accomplish? Is this a failure, a partial success, or something more complicated?

3. **Who bears the cost?** If this institution keeps failing who gets hurt? Does that matter to the people making decisions?

4. **What does this have to do with you?** Is there anything in this article that connects to your life, your community, or the world you expect to inherit?


## {.section-slide background-color="#1B2838"}

::: {.section-slide}
# Phase 2: Prepare Your Verdict

10 minutes
:::


## Presentation Format

One group per article presents — **2–3 minutes.**

- **What happened?** 
- **Your verdict:** is there an institution involved? Is this institution working, failing, or somewhere in between?
- **What surprised you or made you think?** 

Discussion on, especially with paired group


## {.section-slide background-color="#1B2838"}

::: {.section-slide}
# Phase 3: Verdicts
:::


## {.section-slide background-color="#1B2838"}

::: {.section-slide}
# Phase 4: Class Discussion
:::


## Cross-Case Discussion

[Looking across all six cases: is there a common thread? Or is each failure its own story?]{.underline}

- Henkin said states follow international law "almost all of the time." Do these six articles support that? Or do the exceptions matter more than the rule?

- Which of these institutions would you most want to fix? What's the one change that would make the biggest difference?

- [Does international law need to be enforced to matter? Can rules shape behavior even when powerful states ignore them?]{.underline}



## Next Up

- Draft deadline! Beware.

- Last few classes, presentations at the end of the month!


## Questions? {.center}
